evals.prompt: This is a fresh Node.js project. It has a package.json with eslint and prettier set up, but no git hooks at all. I want something to run lint and format on staged files before each commit. Set it up for me.
evals.expected_output: Adds husky + lint-staged to the project, configures a pre-commit hook that runs eslint --fix and prettier --write on staged files, and verifies the setup works.
expectations: Detects that no hook tool exists yet
Chooses husky + lint-staged as the default for a Node-only repo
Reuses existing eslint and prettier instead of inventing new commands
Runs the hook install command and verifies it passes
Does not add pre-commit or lefthook alongside husky

evals.prompt: I have a Python project that already has a .pre-commit-config.yaml with ruff hooks, but it's missing a mypy typecheck hook. Can you add mypy to the pre-commit config while keeping everything else the same?
evals.expected_output: Extends the existing .pre-commit-config.yaml by adding a mypy hook, preserves all existing ruff hooks, and verifies the updated config passes.
expectations: Detects that pre-commit is already the primary tool
Adds mypy as a new hook rather than replacing anything
Does not add husky, lint-staged, or lefthook
Runs pre-commit run --all-files and reports the result

evals.prompt: I have a mixed Node + Python project. There's already a .husky directory with a pre-commit hook, and package.json has some lint-staged config. But the hook doesn't run any Python checks. Can you add Python linting (ruff) and typechecking (mypy) to the commit flow without breaking the existing Node setup?
evals.expected_output: Keeps husky and lint-staged for Node checks, and wires Python checks (ruff, mypy) into the same pre-commit hook or through pre-commit as an orchestrator, without removing or replacing husky.
expectations: Detects that husky is the existing primary tool
Preserves the existing .husky/ setup and lint-staged config
Adds Python checks in a way compatible with the existing Node flow
Does not replace husky with pre-commit or lefthook
Runs the hook tool's validation and both Node and Python checks
